per_record.original_answer: The closure aspect of the Gestalt Principle of Visual Perception is illustrated using a broken circle and a broken square, both represented with dashed lines that are not fully closed. Our perception fills in the gaps to perceive them as complete, whole shapes.

per_record.artifact_only_answer: The slide uses incomplete shapes, specifically a dashed circle and a dashed square, to illustrate the closure aspect of the Gestalt Principle of Visual Perception, demonstrating how our perception fills in missing visual gaps to perceive them as whole forms.

per_record.question: What is the percentage of registered voters who support or lean toward the candidate from the party with the higher total percentage of good policy ideas and high ethical standards and closely follow congressional elections in their district in the survey of U.S. adults conducted April 25 - May 1, 2018?
per_record.original_answer: The Democratic Party has the higher total percentage of good policy ideas and high ethical standards (42% for good policy ideas and 42% for high ethical standards vs. 50% for good policy ideas and 41% for high ethical standards for the GOP, resulting in a tie on policy but a slight edge for Democrats on ethics). Among registered voters, 48% support or lean toward the Democratic candidate. Of those, 52% closely follow congressional elections. Therefore, the percentage of registered voters who support or lean toward the Democratic candidate AND closely follow congressional elections is 48% × 52% = 24.96%, which rounds to 25%.
per_record.artifact_only_answer: The Democratic Party has the higher total percentage (92%) compared to the Republican Party (91%) when combining public views on good policy ideas and high ethical standards. Additionally, 48% of registered voters support or lean toward the Democratic candidate.
